title: Arcytophyllum peruvianum
taxonomy: Arcytophyllum peruvianum is classified as a species of flowering plant in the family Rubiaceae. This family of plants, commonly known as the coffee or madder family, is one of the largest and most diverse families of flowering plants. Arcytophyllum peruvianum is further classified within the genus Arcytophyllum, which is a small genus of flowering plants. The exact classification of Arcytophyllum peruvianum is as follows: Kingdom: Plantae, Clade: Angiosperms, Clade: Eudicots, Clade: Asterids, Order: Gentianales, Family: Rubiaceae, Genus: Arcytophyllum, Species: A. peruvianum. The classification of Arcytophyllum peruvianum provides a clear understanding of its relationships to other plants in the plant kingdom.
uses: Arcytophyllum peruvianum, a plant native to the tropical regions of South America, has been traditionally used in folk medicine for its medicinal properties.
